They bring everyone online.

The basic units of Semble are cards and collections.
The basic activity is bookmarking links and adding them to collections.
And we call those bookmarks cards.
Everything in Semble is on app proto.
Here we're creating a collection.
You can create private collections that only you can add to, or you can create open collections

We also have a search page.

Connections are a way to connect to URLs and let you say something about that connection.

So it's kind of like community notes, but for any URL and for any note.
And we're really excited to see how y'all are going to use it.
We also have some cool notifications in Semble that are a bit different than BlueSky.
So for example, you get notified if someone makes a connection to a card in your library.
That's kind of cool because it's instead of, yeah, it's you're adding your cards and then
you're starting to see how other people build on the knowledge you're adding.
And it's a really interesting way to come across new links.
We also have following, but for collections.
We call it faceted following and we think it's really interesting because it gives you a signal
for specific kinds of content that people are interested in.
So maybe you're creating a bunch of stuff, but you see what people are following.
Maybe they're following your cat video collection, or maybe they're following your research collection.
But it gives you the idea of what are people actually interested in instead of just like
your general stream of content.

Well, we think we're in a unique moment, as Paul was talking about.
And this unique moment is making all our work more urgent and more important, we think.
Because, yeah, the combination of AI and social networks brings great risks and great opportunities.
The value of authentic human connection is going to skyrocket as AI companies are really going
to try to mine our attention and build browsers that are going to record everything we do.
The bright side of this is that AppRoto lets us gain meaningful ownership of our attention.
And this also helps us answer some of the questions people might have at this point, which is, haven't

Semble and @proto let us create circular attention economies.
